Passive immunization using a human monoclonal antibody, m102.4, that targets the ephrin-B2 and ephrin-B3 receptor-binding domain of the henipavirus Nipah G glycoprotein was evaluated in the ferret model as post-exposure prophylaxis.

text: Nipah virus (Henipavirus nipahense) is a bat-borne, zoonotic virus that causes Nipah virus infection in humans and other animals, a disease with a very high case fatality rate (40–75%).

Virology
Like other henipaviruses, the Nipah virus genome is a single (non-segmented) negative-sense, single-stranded RNA of over 18 kb, which is substantially longer than that of other paramyxoviruses.

text: The enveloped virus particles are variable in shape, and can be filamentous or spherical; they contain a helical nucleocapsid.

text: Each strand in the ectodomain consists of four distinct regions: at the N-terminal and connecting to the viral surface is the helical stalk, followed by the beta-sandwich neck domain, the linker region, and finally, at the C-terminal, four heads which contain host cell receptor binding domains.

Emergence
The first cases of Nipah virus infection were identified in 1998, when an outbreak of neurological and respiratory disease on pig farms in peninsular Malaysia caused 265 human cases, with 108 deaths.

Future threat
The Nipah virus has been classified by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) as a Category C agent.

Factors contributing to outbreaks
Population density
The Nipah virus (NiV) has been detected in several of the world's most densely populated areas, particularly in Southeast Asia (SEAR).

text: Nipah virus infection is a zoonotic illness that is transmitted to people from animals and can also be transmitted through contaminated food or directly between people.

text_en: Nipah virus infection is a zoonotic illness that is transmitted to people from animals and can also be transmitted through contaminated food or directly between people.

text: In people with infection, it causes a range of illnesses from asymptomatic infection to acute respiratory illness and brain swelling (encephalitis) for the most severe cases.

text: Cases of Nipah virus infection were first reported in 1998 and since then have been reported in Bangladesh, India, Malaysia, Philippines and Singapore.

text: On 26 January 2026, the India IHR NFP notified WHO of two confirmed NiV infection cases that occurred in West Bengal State.
text_clean: On 26 January 2026, the India IHR NFP notified WHO of two confirmed NiV infection cases that occurred in West Bengal State.
text_en: On 26 January 2026, the India IHR NFP notified WHO of two confirmed NiV infection cases that occurred in West Bengal State.

text: Preliminary laboratory testing suggested NiV infection, and confirmation was received from the National Institute of Virology, Pune on 13 January 2026.
text_clean: Preliminary laboratory testing suggested NiV infection, and confirmation was received from the National Institute of Virology, Pune on 13 January 2026.
text_en: Preliminary laboratory testing suggested NiV infection, and confirmation was received from the National Institute of Virology, Pune on 13 January 2026.

text: The cases were confirmed through Reverse Transcription Polymerase Chain Reaction (RT-PCR) and Enzyme-Linked Immunosorbent Assay (ELISA) testing.
text_clean: The cases were confirmed through Reverse Transcription Polymerase Chain Reaction (RT-PCR) and Enzyme-Linked Immunosorbent Assay (ELISA) testing.
text_en: The cases were confirmed through Reverse Transcription Polymerase Chain Reaction (RT-PCR) and Enzyme-Linked Immunosorbent Assay (ELISA) testing.

text: The first case is a female nurse and the second case is a male nurse.
text_clean: The first case is a female nurse and the second case is a male nurse.
text_en: The first case is a female nurse and the second case is a male nurse.

text: Both cases were between 20 – 30 years old, from Barasat, North 24 Parganas district.
text_clean: Both cases were between 20 30 years old, from Barasat, North 24 Parganas district.
text_en: Both cases were between 20 30 years old, from Barasat, North 24 Parganas district.

text: Both cases developed symptoms typical of severe NiV infection in late December 2025 and were admitted to hospital in early January 2026.
text_clean: Both cases developed symptoms typical of severe NiV infection in late December 2025 and were admitted to hospital in early January 2026.
text_en: Both cases developed symptoms typical of severe NiV infection in late December 2025 and were admitted to hospital in early January 2026.

text: As of 21 January 2026, the second case showed clinical improvement, while the first case remained under critical care.
text_clean: As of 21 January 2026, the second case showed clinical improvement, while the first case remained under critical care.
text_en: As of 21 January 2026, the second case showed clinical improvement, while the first case remained under critical care.

text_en: Following the two confirmed cases, Indian health authorities identified and tested over 190 contact persons, including health and care workers and community contacts.

text_en: All samples from contact persons tested negative for NiV.

text: NiV infection is a zoonotic disease transmitted to humans through infected animals (such as bats), or food contaminated with saliva, urine, and excreta of infected animals.
text_clean: NiV infection is a zoonotic disease transmitted to humans through infected animals (such as bats), or food contaminated with saliva, urine, and excreta of infected animals.
text_en: NiV infection is a zoonotic disease transmitted to humans through infected animals (such as bats), or food contaminated with saliva, urine, and excreta of infected animals.

text: It can also be transmitted directly from person to person through close contact with an infected person.
text_clean: It can also be transmitted directly from person to person through close contact with an infected person.
text_en: It can also be transmitted directly from person to person through close contact with an infected person.

text: Fruit bats or flying foxes (Pteropus species) are the natural hosts for the virus.
text_clean: Fruit bats or flying foxes (Pteropus species) are the natural hosts for the virus.
text_en: Fruit bats or flying foxes (Pteropus species) are the natural hosts for the virus.

text: The incubation period ranges from 3 to 14 days.
text_clean: The incubation period ranges from 3 to 14 days.
text_en: The incubation period ranges from 3 to 14 days.

text: Laboratory diagnosis of a patient with a clinical history of NiV infection can be made during the acute and convalescent phases of the disease by using a combination of tests.
text_clean: Laboratory diagnosis of a patient with a clinical history of NiV infection can be made during the acute and convalescent phases of the disease by using a combination of tests.
text_en: Laboratory diagnosis of a patient with a clinical history of NiV infection can be made during the acute and convalescent phases of the disease by using a combination of tests.

text: The main tests used are RT-PCR from bodily fluids and antibody detection via ELISA.
text_clean: The main tests used are RT-PCR from bodily fluids and antibody detection via ELISA.
text_en: The main tests used are RT-PCR from bodily fluids and antibody detection via ELISA.

text: Human infections range from asymptomatic infection to acute respiratory infection (mild, severe), and fatal encephalitis (brain swelling).
text_clean: Human infections range from asymptomatic infection to acute respiratory infection (mild, severe), and fatal encephalitis (brain swelling).
text_en: Human infections range from asymptomatic infection to acute respiratory infection (mild, severe), and fatal encephalitis (brain swelling).

text: Infected people initially develop symptoms including fever, headaches, myalgia (muscle pain), vomiting and sore throat.
text_clean: Infected people initially develop symptoms including fever, headaches, myalgia (muscle pain), vomiting and sore throat.
text_en: Infected people initially develop symptoms including fever, headaches, myalgia (muscle pain), vomiting and sore throat.

text: Nipah virus (Henipavirus nipahense) is a rare zoonotic pathogen with a high CFR (40-75%) and no licensed vaccine or treatment.

text_en: Nipah virus (Henipavirus nipahense) is a rare zoonotic pathogen with a high CFR (40-75 ) and no licensed vaccine or treatment.

text: Its reservoirs are fruit bats or flying foxes (bats in the Pteropus genus), which are distributed in the coastal regions and on several islands in the Indian ocean, India, south-east Asia and Oceania.
text_clean: Its reservoirs are fruit bats or flying foxes (bats in the Pteropus genus), which are distributed in the coastal regions and on several islands in the Indian ocean, India, south-east Asia and Oceania.
text_en: Its reservoirs are fruit bats or flying foxes (bats in the Pteropus genus), which are distributed in the coastal regions and on several islands in the Indian ocean, India, south-east Asia and Oceania.

text: The virus can be transmitted to humans from wild and domestic animals, however, as the disease can be transmitted by domesticated animals, secondary human-to-human transmissions are also possible.

text_en: The virus can be transmitted to humans from wild and domestic animals, however, as the disease can be transmitted by domesticated animals, secondary human-to-human transmissions are also possible.

text: The virus is present in India, with seasonal outbreaks linked to bat activities and cultural practices such as the consumption of raw date palm sap.
text_clean: The virus is present in India, with seasonal outbreaks linked to bat activities and cultural practices such as the consumption of raw date palm sap.
text_en: The virus is present in India, with seasonal outbreaks linked to bat activities and cultural practices such as the consumption of raw date palm sap.

text: Seasonal outbreaks occur between December and May, coinciding with the harvesting of date palm sap.
text_clean: Seasonal outbreaks occur between December and May, coinciding with the harvesting of date palm sap.
text_en: Seasonal outbreaks occur between December and May, coinciding with the harvesting of date palm sap.

text: This event represents the third Nipah outbreak reported in West Bengal, while multiple Nipah outbreaks were also documented in Kerala since 2018.

text: Based on the current available information, WHO assesses the overall public health risk posed by NiV at the sub-national level to be moderate, taking into consideration no availability of specific drugs or vaccines for NiV infection and the difficulty of early diagnosis.

text: Penularan virus Nipah dapat terjadi melalui beberapa jalur utama:
text_clean: Penularan virus Nipah dapat terjadi melalui beberapa jalur utama:
text_en: Nipah virus transmission can occur through several main routes:

text: Dari Kelelawar ke Manusia: Melalui konsumsi buah atau minuman (seperti air nira) yang telah terkontaminasi air liur, urin, atau kotoran kelelawar.
text_clean: Dari Kelelawar ke Manusia: Melalui konsumsi buah atau minuman (seperti air nira) yang telah terkontaminasi air liur, urin, atau kotoran kelelawar.
text_en: From Bats to Humans: Through consumption of fruit or drinks (such as palm juice) that have been contaminated with bat saliva, urine, or feces.

text: Melalui Inang Perantara (Babi): Manusia dapat tertular melalui kontak langsung dengan babi yang sakit, mengolah daging mentah, atau mengonsumsi daging yang tidak dimasak hingga matang.

text_en: Through Intermediate Hosts (Pigs): Humans can be infected through direct contact with sick pigs, processing raw meat, or consuming meat that is not cooked thoroughly.

text: Antar Manusia: Penularan terjadi melalui kontak erat dengan cairan tubuh pasien yang terinfeksi.
text_clean: Antar Manusia: Penularan terjadi melalui kontak erat dengan cairan tubuh pasien yang terinfeksi.
text_en: Between Humans: Transmission occurs through close contact with the body fluids of infected patients.

text_en: The symptoms of the Nipah virus are often not typical in the early stages, so it is sometimes difficult to notice.

text: Pasien biasanya mengalami demam, sakit kepala, nyeri otot, batuk, nyeri tenggorokan, dan tubuh terasa lemas.

text: The epidemiology, symptoms, diagnosis and management of Nipah virus infections.
text_clean: The epidemiology, symptoms, diagnosis and management of Nipah virus infections.
text_en: The epidemiology, symptoms, diagnosis and management of Nipah virus infections.

text: Nipah virus (NiV) infection is a zoonotic virus (in other words, it can be transmitted from animals to humans).
text_clean: Nipah virus (NiV) infection is a zoonotic virus (in other words, it can be transmitted from animals to humans).
text_en: Nipah virus (NiV) infection is a zoonotic virus (in other words, it can be transmitted from animals to humans).

text: It can also be transmitted through contaminated food or directly from human-to-human.
text_clean: It can also be transmitted through contaminated food or directly from human-to-human.
text_en: It can also be transmitted through contaminated food or directly from human-to-human.

text: NiV is a member of the family Paramyxoviridae, genus Henipavirus.
text_clean: NiV is a member of the family Paramyxoviridae, genus Henipavirus.
text_en: NiV is a member of the family Paramyxoviridae, genus Henipavirus.

text: The natural reservoir of NiV is bats, particularly fruit bats of the Pteropus genus.
text_clean: The natural reservoir of NiV is bats, particularly fruit bats of the Pteropus genus.
text_en: The natural reservoir of NiV is bats, particularly fruit bats of the Pteropus genus.

text: NiV was initially isolated and identified in 1999 during an outbreak of encephalitis and respiratory illness among pig farmers and people with close contact with pigs in Malaysia and Singapore.
text_clean: NiV was initially isolated and identified in 1999 during an outbreak of encephalitis and respiratory illness among pig farmers and people with close contact with pigs in Malaysia and Singapore.
text_en: NiV was initially isolated and identified in 1999 during an outbreak of encephalitis and respiratory illness among pig farmers and people with close contact with pigs in Malaysia and Singapore.

text: Outbreaks have occurred subsequently in parts of Northeast India and almost annually since 2001 in specific districts in Bangladesh.
text_clean: Outbreaks have occurred subsequently in parts of Northeast India and almost annually since 2001 in specific districts in Bangladesh.
text_en: Outbreaks have occurred subsequently in parts of Northeast India and almost annually since 2001 in specific districts in Bangladesh.

text: More recently, cases have been reported in Kerala in Southern India following its first outbreak of NiV infection in 2018.
text_clean: More recently, cases have been reported in Kerala in Southern India following its first outbreak of NiV infection in 2018.
text_en: More recently, cases have been reported in Kerala in Southern India following its first outbreak of NiV infection in 2018.

text: Cases of Henipavirus infection have also occurred in the Philippines, believed to have been caused by NiV or a Nipah-like virus.
text_clean: Cases of Henipavirus infection have also occurred in the Philippines, believed to have been caused by NiV or a Nipah-like virus.
text_en: Cases of Henipavirus infection have also occurred in the Philippines, believed to have been caused by NiV or a Nipah-like virus.

text: NiV has been isolated from the urine of bats in Malaysia, and antibodies against NiV have been detected in 23 species of bat across Asia, Ghana and Madagascar.
text_clean: NiV has been isolated from the urine of bats in Malaysia, and antibodies against NiV have been detected in 23 species of bat across Asia, Ghana and Madagascar.
text_en: NiV has been isolated from the urine of bats in Malaysia, and antibodies against NiV have been detected in 23 species of bat across Asia, Ghana and Madagascar.

text: However, human outbreaks of NiV infection have not been identified outside South and South East Asia, and most outbreaks have occurred in rural or semi-rural locations.
text_clean: However, human outbreaks of NiV infection have not been identified outside South and South East Asia, and most outbreaks have occurred in rural or semi-rural locations.
text_en: However, human outbreaks of NiV infection have not been identified outside South and South East Asia, and most outbreaks have occurred in rural or semi-rural locations.

text: See the HCID: country specific risk webpage for further information on cases.
text_clean: See the HCID: country specific risk webpage for further information on cases.
text_en: See the HCID: country specific risk webpage for further information on cases.

text: The 1998 Malaysian outbreak occurred following a spill-over event.
text_clean: The 1998 Malaysian outbreak occurred following a spill-over event.
text_en: The 1998 Malaysian outbreak occurred following a spill-over event.

text: NiV from bats spread to pigs, followed by transmission to humans exposed to the infected urine and/or respiratory secretions of infected pigs.
text_clean: NiV from bats spread to pigs, followed by transmission to humans exposed to the infected urine and/or respiratory secretions of infected pigs.
text_en: NiV from bats spread to pigs, followed by transmission to humans exposed to the infected urine and/or respiratory secretions of infected pigs.

text: Other outbreaks have been associated with consumption or collection of foodstuffs, such as raw or partially fermented date palm sap, which were contaminated with bat saliva and/or excreta containing NiV.
text_clean: Other outbreaks have been associated with consumption or collection of foodstuffs, such as raw or partially fermented date palm sap, which were contaminated with bat saliva and/or excreta containing NiV.
text_en: Other outbreaks have been associated with consumption or collection of foodstuffs, such as raw or partially fermented date palm sap, which were contaminated with bat saliva and/or excreta containing NiV.

text: Human-to-human transmission of NiV  in Bangladesh and India has been reported previously.
text_clean: Human-to-human transmission of NiV in Bangladesh and India has been reported previously.
text_en: Human-to-human transmission of NiV in Bangladesh and India has been reported previously.

text: This is most commonly seen in the family and close contacts of NiV infected cases.
text_clean: This is most commonly seen in the family and close contacts of NiV infected cases.
text_en: This is most commonly seen in the family and close contacts of NiV infected cases.

text: Close and direct, unprotected contact with infected patients, especially those with respiratory symptoms, has been implicated as a transmission risk.
text_clean: Close and direct, unprotected contact with infected patients, especially those with respiratory symptoms, has been implicated as a transmission risk.
text_en: Close and direct, unprotected contact with infected patients, especially those with respiratory symptoms, has been implicated as a transmission risk.

text: NiV ribonucleic acid (RNA) has been detected in semen from an individual who received treatment for NiV infection, with RNA detected at day 26 following onset of illness (but not at days 42 or 59).
text_clean: NiV ribonucleic acid (RNA) has been detected in semen from an individual who received treatment for NiV infection, with RNA detected at day 26 following onset of illness (but not at days 42 or 59).

text: These findings suggest that there may be a potential risk of sexual transmission of NiV, although the viability of NiV detected in the individual’s semen remains unknown, and sexual transmission of NiV has not been described to date.

text: There is evidence that NiV can infect other animals, including dogs, cats, goats, horses and sheep.

text: The most important complication of NiV infection is encephalitis, which is associated with a high mortality rate; however, the full spectrum of clinical illness is not completely understood.
text_clean: The most important complication of NiV infection is encephalitis, which is associated with a high mortality rate; however, the full spectrum of clinical illness is not completely understood.
text_en: The most important complication of NiV infection is encephalitis, which is associated with a high mortality rate; however, the full spectrum of clinical illness is not completely understood.

text: The incubation period is presumed to be 4 to 21 days, however longer incubation periods have rarely been observed in previous outbreaks.
text_clean: The incubation period is presumed to be 4 to 21 days, however longer incubation periods have rarely been observed in previous outbreaks.
text_en: The incubation period is presumed to be 4 to 21 days, however longer incubation periods have rarely been observed in previous outbreaks.

text: Typically, patients present with a sudden onset, non-specific flu-like or febrile illness.

text: Encephalitis or meningitis are a key hallmark of NiV infection, with associated symptoms usually developing after 3 to 21 days from illness onset.

text_en: Encephalitis or meningitis are a key hallmark of NiV infection, with associated symptoms usually developing after 3 to 21 days from illness onset.

text_en: Cerebrospinal fluid abnormalities are similar to those seen in other acute viral central nervous system infections.

text: NiV case fatality rates have varied between outbreaks and appear to be related to the strain of infection, but it is high overall (40 to 75%).
